title: Apps
catalogDescription: Community-verified apps that work on Meta Portal devices
visitorCatalogDescription: Browse community-verified apps before connecting a Meta Portal
visitorConnectHint: You can browse the catalog without a device. Connect your Portal when you want to install an app or check what is already installed.
dropToInstall: Drop the .apk file to install it
dragDropApk: Drag & drop an APK file here, or click to browse
readmeError: Couldn't load the README from GitHub
notAnApk: That file is not an APK
dragDropToInstall: Drag & drop the APK to install
uninstallConfirm: Are you sure you want to uninstall {{name}}?
manageInstalledDesc: Manage apps installed on your Portal
clearDataConfirm: Clear all data and cache for {{name}}? You can't undo this.

gphotos.intro: Show your Google Photos albums as a screensaver. This needs your own Google Cloud OAuth credentials.
gphotos.step1: Create a Google Cloud project and enable the Photos Picker API.
gphotos.step2: Configure the OAuth consent screen (External) and add yourself as a test user.
gphotos.step3: Create a Desktop OAuth client and download its client_secret.json.
gphotos.done: Screensaver configured — finish signing in on the Portal.

portalCalendar.intro: Turn your Portal into a wall calendar. You set it up from your browser.
portalCalendar.step1: Add your calendars as iCal feeds (Google, iCloud, or any .ics URL) and give each one a color and a name.
portalCalendar.step2: Optionally connect iCloud or Google for two-way sync so you can create events from the Portal.
portalCalendar.step3: Turn on "Show the calendar when the Portal idles" to use it as a screensaver.
portalCalendar.addressLabel: Open this address in your browser
portalCalendar.addressHint: Use a phone or computer on the same Wi-Fi as your Portal.
portalCalendar.noAddress: Couldn't find the Portal's network address. Make sure it's connected to Wi-Fi.
